examples.title: Linear List Directory Search
examples.description: Demonstrates sequential search in linear list directory implementation
examples.code:
FUNCTION LinearSearch(directory, targetName)
  FOR each entry IN directory
    IF entry.name == targetName THEN
      RETURN entry  // Found
    END IF
  END FOR
  RETURN NULL  // Not found
END FUNCTION

// Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of entries
examples.explanation: This algorithm searches through directory entries one by one until the target file is found or list ends. Simple but inefficient for large directories.

examples.title: Hash Table Directory Implementation
examples.description: Shows hash table based directory with collision handling
examples.explanation: Hash table uses hash function to compute index. Collisions handled via chaining. Provides fast average-case lookup.

examples.title: Path Resolution Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates how absolute and relative paths are resolved
examples.code:
FUNCTION ResolvePath(currentDir, path)
  IF path[0] == '/' THEN  // Absolute path
    current = rootDirectory
    startIndex = 1
  ELSE  // Relative path
    current = currentDir
    startIndex = 0
  END IF
  
  components = Split(path, '/', startIndex)
  
  FOR each component IN components
    IF component == '.' THEN
      CONTINUE  // Current directory
    ELSE IF component == '..' THEN
      current = current.parent  // Go to parent
    ELSE IF component == '' THEN
      CONTINUE  // Skip multiple slashes
    ELSE
      IF current.children.contains(component) THEN
        current = current.children[component]
      ELSE
        RETURN ERROR  // Path not found
      END IF
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  RETURN current  // Resolved directory
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This algorithm handles both absolute and relative paths, processing components like '.' (current), '..' (parent), and regular directory names to navigate the directory tree.

examples.title: Directory Entry Structure (FCB)
examples.description: File Control Block structure representing directory entry
examples.explanation: This C structure represents a directory entry (FCB) storing file metadata. Inodes in actual systems contain additional pointers to data blocks for file content storage.
